complexity.rationale: Pure utility domain. No complex business rules, no external service deps, no domain events. The main complexity is the ResourceName-completeness invariant (auto-create all language rows on insert) and the caching pattern. Both are straightforward. DB schema is simple. The frontend integration adds a little complexity (BiHttpTranslateLoader, key-casing convention) but nothing architecturally challenging.

business_value.rationale: Every user-facing string in the system flows through this domain. It underpins the entire multi-language product (DK, SE, EN, FI, NO — and partially DE). Without it, the system cannot display language-appropriate content for any country. Critical path for all UI rendering.

rebuild_risk.rationale: Low risk. The domain has a clean service interface (ILocalizationService), no circular dependencies, and no complex state machine. The DB schema is stable. The main risk is ensuring the ResourceName-completeness invariant is preserved in the new implementation.

inbound_strong: StatusController — uses GetLocalizedResource for status description translation
MessageController — uses GetLocalizedResource for default group name and datetime format
UserController — uses GetLocalizedResource for login error message
CommonController — owns the HTTP CRUD surface
BiHttpTranslateLoader (Angular) — loads entire language dictionary on bootstrap
FormattedExcelReportDownloader — uses ILocalizationService for file naming
inbound_weak: Any component using NGX-translate keys via BiTranslateService
coupling.coupling_note: High INBOUND coupling — this domain is relied upon by many others. Outbound coupling is simple (DB + cache only). This makes it safe to refactor internally without breaking callers, as long as ILocalizationService interface is preserved.

open_gaps_summary.description: Batch import does not invalidate cache. One-line fix.
open_gaps_summary.recommendation: Fix immediately in sms-service. No design decision needed.

open_gaps_summary.description: Countries table is a dead table. All mappings in CountryConstants.cs.
open_gaps_summary.recommendation: Document as accepted technical debt. If green-ai rebuild is planned, use rebuild as the opportunity to either drop the table or consolidate.

open_gaps_summary.description: German (DE=6) missing from BiLanguageId frontend enum and BiHttpTranslateLoader.
open_gaps_summary.recommendation: Needs business decision: is German market actively served on this frontend? If yes, add BiLanguageId.DE=6, add 'de' → 6 mapping in BiHttpTranslateLoader, add German option to translation-management initLanguageOptions().

open_gaps_summary.description: updateLocalizedResource in TranslationManagementService hardcodes 'da'/'se' for frontend cache update.
open_gaps_summary.recommendation: Replace with getLanguageCodeByLanguageId(languageId) — same pattern already used in insertLocalizedResources. One-line fix.

quick_wins: Fix GAP_005 (TranslationManagementService.updateLocalizedResource hardcode) — 1 line, zero risk
Fix GAP_002 (ImportLocaleStringResourcesAsync cache invalidation) — 1 line, eliminates silent import failures

migration_recommendation.description: Port ILocalizationService and DB schema as-is to green-ai. Fix GAP_002 and GAP_004 during port. Rebuild admin UI in Blazor as full server-side paginated page. Drop Countries table (GAP_003) if architecture decision confirms no dynamic country config is needed.
do_not_port: TranslationManagementService (Angular-specific, replace with Blazor admin page)
